title: vitis vulpina var. amurensis
intro: Vitis vulpina var. amurensis, also known as Amur grape or Amur wild grape, is a species of grapevine native to East Asia, characterized by its small, round fruits and woody stems. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Vitis vulpina var. amurensis include cardiovascular protection, improved cognitive function, and enhanced immune system function. Herbal preparations of Vitis vulpina var. amurensis can be found in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and extracts, which are often used to promote overall well-being and support the treatment of various health conditions.
